expertAssessments.rationale: The change is a patch-level React and React DOM dependency alignment; installed package versions, import smoke, and root type-check passed.
Validation used a small high-signal set for a package-only patch and intentionally avoided broad test coverage chasing or live external QA.

completedImprovements.title: Align root React and React DOM to 19.2.6
completedImprovements.evidence: react and react-dom updated from 19.2.4 to 19.2.6; npm registry latest confirmed as 19.2.6; import smoke, root type-check, and git diff whitespace check passed.

notes: npm install react@^19.2.6 react-dom@^19.2.6 changed 2 packages.
npm ls react react-dom --depth=0 showed react@19.2.6 and react-dom@19.2.6.
npm audit --omit=dev still exits non-zero only for Next.js high advisories. This is the existing upstream patch wait item, not a React regression.
npm run test:quick and live QA were intentionally skipped per the user's cost/time guidance because this is a package-only patch-level dependency change.
